name: Argo Ship
description: The Argo was the ship on which Jason and the Argonauts sailed from Iolcos to Colchis to retrieve the Golden Fleece. It was specially built by Argus, with Athena's guidance, and was remarkably strong and swift. The ship was said to have been crafted from timber cut from Mount Pelion. A piece of timber from the sacred grove of Dodona, which possessed the power of speech and prophecy, was incorporated into the ship's prow, allowing it to offer advice and warnings to the crew. The Argo faced numerous trials during its voyage, including navigating through the Symplegades (Clashing Rocks), enduring storms orchestrated by Hera, and escaping the dangers of Sirens and Scylla and Charybdis. After the successful return of Jason and the Argonauts, the Argo was dedicated to Poseidon and was placed among the stars as the constellation Argo Navis (now divided into several constellations). It symbolized adventure, courage, and the pursuit of impossible quests. Its destruction is debated, some accounts say it crumbled with age while others state it was destroyed in an accident involving Jason.
subtitle: Jason's legendary ship of adventure
material: Timber from Mount Pelion and Dodona oak
origin: Built by Argus with Athena's guidance for Jason's quest.

keyMyths.title: The Quest for the Golden Fleece
keyMyths.description: Jason and the Argonauts sailed on the Argo to Colchis to retrieve the Golden Fleece, facing numerous challenges and monsters along the way. The Argo's construction and the Dodona oak prow were key to their success.

keyMyths.title: The Symplegades
keyMyths.description: The Argo successfully navigated the Symplegades, or Clashing Rocks, by releasing a dove to test the passage, then rowing swiftly through as the rocks recoiled. This demonstrates the ship's speed and the crew's bravery.

sources.author: Apollonius Rhodius
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Jason and the Argonauts' voyage.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Jason and the Argonauts' voyage.

sources.author: Hyginus
